activated_rules.rule_name: PDF with multiple documents; identify correct document pair and use Original certificate
Consignor search across all invoice files/pages
Goods description verification by controlled text match / article identifiers in description
Origin must be explicitly stated in invoice

consignor.motivation: Original certificate page 1 states consignor 'Components, Box 124, 512 23 Göteborg, Sweden'. The invoice identified for pairing is the 2-page invoice in Document A, where page 1 header explicitly contains the same company name and country: 'Components, Box 124, 512 23 Göteborg, Sweden'. Exact identification is possible after minor punctuation/spacing normalization, so consignor and country are verified.

consignee.motivation: The certificate consignee is 'Bluesafe, Coenecoop 3D2, 2741PG Waddinxveen, Netherlands'. The paired invoice shows the same party in the buyer/address block and delivery address on page 1. Company name and country match explicitly; spacing difference in postcode does not affect the result.

goods_description.motivation: The certificate lists six goods lines with article-identifying HS/item headings and identity-bearing descriptions. In the paired invoice, each listed heading/description can be identified explicitly: 6402911000, 6403400000, 6403911690 with 'ELITE Hoge veiligheidsschoen'; 6403999390 with 'STORM Lage Veiligheidsschoen'; and 6404199000 with both 'STORM Veiligheidsschoen Zwart/Ge...' and 'STORM Hoge Veiligheidsschoen'. The invoice contains additional suffix '(LK)'/origin text and truncation 'Zwart/Ge', which do not negate identification of the certificate descriptions. No article numbers in the certificate fail identification in the invoice.

rules_applied.outcome: MATCH for 257 and 86 via same-invoice row summation by same goods grouping

quantity.motivation: The certificate quantity field contains six explicit pair quantities. Four quantities (296, 185, 968, 464) are explicitly present in invoice subtotal rows for the corresponding goods groups. For the two certificate lines under HS 6404199000, the invoice contains two distinct product groupings under the same heading: 'STORM Veiligheidsschoen Zwart/Ge...' with row quantities summing to 257, and 'STORM Hoge Veiligheidsschoen' with row quantities summing to 86. This fits the same-invoice summation exception for corresponding grouped goods lines. No explicit contradictory quantity for the same grouped goods was found.

origin.motivation: The certificate states origin 'Sri Lanka'. The paired invoice explicitly states 'Sri Lanka' repeatedly in the goods sections on both pages. The origin is therefore explicitly identifiable in the invoice.

overall_assessment.decision_summary: The correct document pair is the Original certificate in Document B pages 1-2 and the invoice in Document A. Document B also contains a Copy certificate and a different invoice, but under rule 8.5 the verification must use the consistent pair. The Original certificate references order/article remark '5978851 / 8164047', matches consignee Bluesafe, and its consignor 'Components' is explicitly present in the paired invoice header. All applicable control points are verified as MATCH under the stated rules; therefore the document pair comparison result is IDENTICAL and eligible for auto approval.
